Off Calendar Mar-15-2022 Continued To Apr-12-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Laura Peebles, via ZOOM, for Charlie Turman III, via ZOOM, Administrator.

The court confirms with Ms. Peebles that the transfer of remaining funds has not been done yet. Ms. Peebles reports that her client plans to transfer the funds today and asks for a continuance to allow time to complete the task and file proof of the deposit with the court.

The Status on Filing of Receipts, etc., and Status on Distribution of the Reserve are continued to Apr-12-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: C. Gomez. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Distribution Of The Reserve
Off Calendar Sep-21-2021 Continued To Dec-07-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lauren Peebles appearedby zoom for and with Charlie Turman by zoom.

The court noted that this should be a pretty straight forward hearing, that it spent more than three hours preparing for this hearing because what was filed and what was needed are worlds apart, and referred to the September 24, 2019 order. Ms. Peebles stated that a distribution amount of $580,200.00 is what was represented to her. The court went over the numbers that were in the accounting, noted that it wanted receipts/proof of where the money went, and asked Mr. Turman why he wrote distribution checks to himself for the benefit of his father, instead of directly to the father. Mr. Turman stated that he did that because he had ignorance of this process, that his father did not want to close the estate, that he was doing what his father asked him to do and that he wrote checks to the IRS for his father. The court stated that it needs some proof that the funds ended up with the father, that it needs copies of cancelled checks, that it needs proof of how much was in the father's estate in August, and that it needs support for anything that Mr. Turman paid for his dad and it needs to come up to $580,000.00. The court also stated that it needs cancelled checks showing payments of $242,555.00 to the IRS and that it needs proof of what is left. Mr. Turman stated that nothing is left in the brother's estate, but then when the court noted that the accounting seems to show that $120,000.00 was left in the brother's estate in 2020, he stated that it is still the brother's account. The court informed Mr. Turman that he needs to move $120,000.00 from the brother's estate account to the father's estate account. The court informed Mr. Turman that it needs proof that the funds in Julius' estate went to where the court ordered it to go in 2020 and clarified that it is asking for a copy of the letters from Charlie Jr.'s estate in Alabama. Ms. Peebles assured the court that she will work with her client to get what is needed. Status on distribution of the reserve, and status on filing of the receipts for distribution ordered in 2019 are continued to December 7, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Receipts For Distributions Ordered In September 2019
Off Calendar Dec-22-2020 Continued To Feb-23-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lauren Peebles, Esq., counsel for Charlie Turman III, Administrator, (appearing by video conference).

The Court recites the matters before the Court, noting that the information provided was somewhat conclusory as it offered no specific detail as to how the distributions were made or evidence, such as cashed checks, that the distributions were actually received by the distributee.

The Court also notes that there was no detail as to how the reserve account was depleted, only that the $150,000 in the reserve account was spent. The Court will require a more detailed, if informal, accounting for the expenses of the reserve account.

Ms. Peebles notes that as the administrator is out of state, it is often difficult to reach him. She also advises the Court that she hopes to have the letters for the deceased distributee's estate shortly [the sole beneficiary of the estate died shortly after distribution].

The matter is continued to February 23, 2021 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. Ms. Peebles must file the requested documentation and deliver endorsed-filed copies to the Examiner on or before February 16, 2021.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Bailiff: Deputy Ruiz; Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Receipts For Distributions Ordered In September 2019
Off Calendar Nov-03-2020 Continued To Dec-01-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lauren Peebles, Esq., counsel for and with Charlie Turman III, personal representative (appearing separately by video conference, Mr. Turman audio only).

The Court notes a want of explanation with respect to distribution of the estate to the beneficiary and as to the status of the reserve. Ms. Peebles advises the Court that delay in reporting the status of reserve was occasioned by the delay in filing final tax returns for the estate - which was completed this week - and that the reserve has now been exhausted. As for the distribution of the estate, the distribution was made to the estate beneficiary who died before signing the receipt on distribution. The beneficiary/decedent's estate is now open in Alabama, letters issued, and the personal representative for that estate should now be authorized to sign the receipt.

The Court notes that a declaration evidencing these developments might also suffice to alleviate some of the Court's concerns. At the Court's invitation, Ms. Peebles reports that she should have all of the necessary documentation available within 30 days. The Court continues the matter to December 1, 2020 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. The required receipts or other supporting documentation must be filed on or before November 24, 2020.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Deputy: K. Kabasares; Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Distribution Of The Reserve

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Distribution Of The Reserve
Off Calendar Sep-08-2020 Continued To Nov-03-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lauren Peebles appeared by zoom for and with Charlie Turman by zoom.

The Court stated that it is waiting for the receipts on distribution of the reserve and that it does not have receipts for the distributions that were ordered in September of 2019. Ms. Peebles stated that they do not have the receipts available, that the CPA retired earlier this year and that the Administrator is working with the CPA's replacement. Ms. Peebles also requested an eight-week continuance to see where the new CPA is in the process and stated that they will then make distribution to the Administrator and his Father. Status on distribution of the reserve is continued to November 3, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on filing of the receipts for distributions ordered in September 2019 is also set for November 3, 2020.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-24-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lauren Peebles appeared by telephone for Charlie Turman.

The Court noted that there is a request for a very substantial reserve and stated that it understands it is for potential tax liabilities. Ms. Peebles stated that the amount of the tax liability still remains uncertain at this time, that the CPA has re-calculated the taxes and that originally the amount was higher than what it ultimately will be. Ms. Peebles also stated that the CPA re-calculated that approximately $90,000.00 will go to the Internal Revenue Service, that $50,000.00 will go to the State, that the tax forms are not available to the CPA until January of 2020 and that they would ask for a reserve of $150,000.00 to cover the tax preparation fees. The First and Final Report on Waiver of Account, etc. is under submission. Status on distribution of the reserve is set for June 16, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-20-2019 Continued To Sep-17-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lauren Peebles appeared by telephone for Charlie Turman III (Administrator).

The Court noted that the Probate Examiner's notes have not been responded to. Ms. Peebles apologized to the Court and requested a continuance stating that the Administrator is working with the Decedent's CPA to confirm that all of the applicable income taxes have been paid, that they have requested a reserve for the purpose of making sure that taxes and expenses are paid, but they have to confirm what an appropriate amount would be and that they are waiting to receive that information from the CPA. The First and Final Report on Waiver of Account, etc. is continued to September 17, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court stated that it expects to have a response to the Examiner's notes by then, and that if the matter is not ready to proceed, Counsel withdraws this petition and files a petition for approval of a report on status of administration.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.
